title: Barbier Coupling Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Barbier coupling reaction describing in situ generation of organometallic reagents in the presence of carbonyl compounds to furnish alcohols after workup. The page notes mechanistic overlap with Grignard chemistry and discusses both concerted and radical stepwise pathways.
notes: Page emphasizes operational simplicity, aqueous compatibility for some metals, and immediate trapping of unstable organometallic species by aldehydes or ketones.

entities.notes: Overview explicitly compares Barbier to Grignard chemistry.
Mechanistic chart presents SET pathway for organometallic generation.
Canonical outcome after carbonyl addition and workup.

similar_existing.transformation_type: in situ organometallic generation from alkyl/allyl halides with direct addition to carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: The Barbier reaction generates an organometallic reagent in the presence of a carbonyl compound so that addition occurs immediately without prior isolation of the Grignard-like species.

similar_existing.transformation_type: beta-keto ester / dicarbonyl cleavage
similar_existing.description_short: Mentioned in color-explanation front matter on page xiv.

similar_existing.transformation_type: ortho formylation of phenols and heteroarenes via dichlorocarbene chemistry
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational dichlorocarbene-based formylation method for phenols and related heteroarenes that usually delivers ortho-aldehydes but can also produce abnormal rearrangement products.

similar_existing.transformation_type: other
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: MVK; CH3CN | MVK; CH3CN / H2O | Mg(ClO4)2; chiral bis-oxazoline ligand; DCM
similar_existing.description_short: Aza-ene reaction

similar_existing.transformation_type: other

title: Birch Reduction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Birch reduction describing dissolving-metal reduction of aromatic rings or electron-poor heteroarenes with alkali metals in liquid ammonia and an alcohol proton source to give unconjugated cyclohexadienes or partially reduced heterocycles. The mechanism proceeds through sequential electron transfer, radical anion formation, protonation, and regiocontrolled second electron/proton delivery.
notes: Highlights substituent-dependent regioselectivity, use of Li or Na in NH3(l), and applicability to pyridines, furans, and related systems under electron-withdrawing substitution.

entities.notes: First electron-transfer intermediate in Birch reduction.
Typical partially reduced arene product class.
Variant that traps reduced intermediates with electrophiles.

similar_existing.transformation_type: dissolving-metal dearomatization / reductive alkylation
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: Li or Na, NH3(l), ROH, t-BuOH, EtOH
similar_existing.description_short: Dissolving-metal reduction of aromatic rings and heteroarenes to unconjugated dienes or partially reduced heterocycles.

similar_existing.transformation_type: CeCl3/NaBH4 reduction of alpha,beta-unsaturated ketones to allylic alcohols
similar_existing.description_short: Selective 1,2-reduction of enones to allylic alcohols using sodium borohydride in the presence of cerium(III) chloride.

similar_existing.transformation_type: other
similar_existing.description_short: The Buchner reaction, involving the thermal or photochemical reaction of ethyl diazoacetate with benzene to form a cycloheptatriene derivative via a norcaradiene intermediate.

similar_existing.transformation_type: aminomethylation of enolizable carbonyls or other CH-acidic partners with formaldehyde and amines
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational carbon–carbon bond-forming reaction that installs aminomethyl substituents onto enolizable carbonyl systems via iminium intermediates.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Lewis-acid-mediated propargylic substitution/decomplexation of cobalt-alkyne complexes
similar_existing.description_short: Cobalt-complex-mediated propargylic substitution method that enables controlled generation and trapping of stabilized propargylic cations.

title: Carroll Rearrangement (Kimel-Cope Rearrangement) – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Carroll rearrangement describing [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of allylic beta-keto esters to gamma,delta-unsaturated ketones, often followed by decarboxylation, and conceptually related to a decarboxylative Claisen rearrangement.
notes: Key concepts: ester enolate, [3,3]-sigmatropic shift, decarboxylation, gamma-delta unsaturated ketone formation.

entities.notes: Canonical substrate class for the Carroll/Kimel-Cope rearrangement.
Concerted rearrangement step explicitly shown on the overview page.
Characteristic product class formed after rearrangement and decarboxylation.

similar_existing.transformation_type: decarboxylative Claisen-type [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: allylic beta-keto esters, ester enolates, heat, LDA, decarboxylation
similar_existing.description_short: Rearrangement of allylic beta-keto esters to gamma,delta-unsaturated ketones via a [3,3]-sigmatropic shift and loss of carbon dioxide.

similar_existing.transformation_type: 1,5-diene [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement / ring expansion of strained dienes
similar_existing.description_short: Thermal reversible [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of 1,5-dienes that reorganizes carbon skeletons, including medium-ring-forming strained-substrate variants.

similar_existing.transformation_type: anionic silyl migration / annulation
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: alkoxide or organolithium generation, acyl silanes, THF
similar_existing.description_short: Anionic migration of silyl groups from carbon to oxygen that enables rearrangement and annulation logic.

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement of alpha-diazoketones to ketenes
similar_existing.description_short: Conversion of alpha-diazoketones into ketenes with accompanying 1,2-shift, widely used for ring contraction and homologation chemistry.

similar_existing.transformation_type: 4-carbonyl-substituted oxazole isomerization / heterocycle skeletal editing
similar_existing.description_short: Thermal rearrangement of 4-carbonyl-substituted oxazoles through dicarbonylnitrile ylide intermediates to isomeric oxazole products and related heterocycle-edited scaffolds.

title: Amadori Reaction / Rearrangement – Importance, Mechanism, and Initial Application
summary: Overview page for the Amadori rearrangement describing conversion of N-glycosides to 1-amino-1-deoxyketoses under acid or Lewis-acid conditions, with mechanistic ring opening, enol formation, and tautomerization. Includes an initial synthetic application to D-fructose analogs.
notes: Page links the reaction to the Maillard reaction and non-enzymatic browning chemistry.

entities.notes: Explicitly referenced as broader carbohydrate rearrangement context.
Initial application shown on p14.

similar_existing.transformation_type: N-glycoside to 1-amino-1-deoxyketose rearrangement
similar_existing.description_short: The Amadori rearrangement converts N-glycosides into 1-amino-1-deoxyketoses and is a central transformation in carbohydrate chemistry and Maillard-type processes.

title: Arbuzov Reaction (Michaelis–Arbuzov Reaction) – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Arbuzov reaction showing transformation of alkyl halides and trialkyl phosphites into dialkyl phosphonates or related organophosphorus derivatives through phosphonium-salt formation and rearrangement.
notes: Page explicitly connects the chemistry to Horner-Wadsworth-Emmons olefination and phosphonate synthesis.

entities.notes: Overview page explicitly connects Arbuzov phosphonates to HWE chemistry.

similar_existing.transformation_type: alkyl halide to phosphonate via phosphite rearrangement
similar_existing.description_short: The Arbuzov reaction transforms alkyl halides and phosphites into phosphonates or related organophosphorus products by way of phosphonium intermediates and alkyl migration.

title: Aza-Claisen Rearrangement (3-Aza-Cope Rearrangement) – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the aza-Claisen rearrangement, the thermal [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of N-allyl enamines, with comparison to neutral, charged, and keteneimine manifolds and a chairlike transition state.
notes: Also includes a synthetic application to (-)-isoiridomyrmecin from carbamide enolate rearrangement.

entities.notes: Overview compares aza-Claisen directly to Claisen.
Synthetic application shown at bottom of p20.

similar_existing.transformation_type: thermal [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of N-allyl enamines
similar_existing.description_short: The aza-Claisen rearrangement is the [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of N-allyl enamines and related aza-hexadiene systems, often used for stereocontrolled alkaloid synthesis.

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement
similar_existing.description_short: Named reaction family listed in the alphabetical reaction index pages of the source book.

title: Baeyer-Villiger Oxidation/Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Baeyer-Villiger oxidation/rearrangement describing oxidation of ketones to esters and cyclic ketones to lactones or hydroxy acids by peroxyacids. The page summarizes migratory aptitude, stereochemical retention at the migrating carbon, and the Criegee-intermediate mechanism.
notes: Mechanism panel features peroxyacid addition to a ketone, Criegee intermediate formation, migration anti to the peroxide leaving group, and ester/lactone formation; page lists common oxidants such as mCPBA and peracetic acid.

entities.notes: Named mechanistic intermediate on the overview page.
Canonical cyclic-ketone oxidation product.

similar_existing.transformation_type: ketone to ester or lactone via peroxyacid oxidation
similar_existing.description_short: Baeyer-Villiger oxidation inserts oxygen next to a carbonyl through Criegee-intermediate formation and alkyl migration, transforming ketones into esters or lactones.

oxidation

title: Criegee Oxidation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Criegee oxidation describing oxidative cleavage of vicinal diols to carbonyl compounds with lead tetraacetate under anhydrous organic conditions, including cyclic and acyclic substrates and mechanistic pathways involving lead glycolate intermediates.
notes: Key concepts: vicinal diol cleavage, Pb(OAc)4-mediated oxidation, lead glycolate intermediates, carbonyl-fragment generation from cyclic and acyclic 1,2-diols.

entities.notes: Named vicinal-diol oxidative cleavage family overview.
Mechanistic lead-bound glycolate species proposed before C-C bond fragmentation.
Canonical cleavage products explicitly shown on the overview page.

similar_existing.transformation_type: vicinal diol oxidative cleavage to carbonyl fragments
similar_existing.description_short: Oxidative cleavage of vicinal diols to carbonyl compounds using lead tetraacetate, often as a mild alternative for advanced cyclic diol fragmentation.

similar_existing.transformation_type: TPAP/NMO oxidation of primary and secondary alcohols to aldehydes and ketones
similar_existing.description_short: Mild ruthenate-catalyzed alcohol oxidation that converts sensitive primary and secondary alcohols into aldehydes and ketones using TPAP and NMO.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd/Cu-catalyzed oxidation of alkenes to carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Palladium-catalyzed oxidation of alkenes, especially terminal alkenes, to carbonyl compounds including methyl ketones and related oxidative cyclization products.

similar_existing.transformation_type: primary/secondary alcohol oxidation to aldehyde or ketone
similar_existing.description_short: Mild oxidation of alcohols to aldehydes or ketones using the NCS/DMS/Et3N reagent system via alkoxysulfonium intermediates.

similar_existing.transformation_type: palladium-mediated dehydrogenation of silyl enol ethers to enones and enals
similar_existing.description_short: Named oxidation that converts silyl enol ethers into conjugated enones or enals under Pd(II) conditions.

title: Curtius Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Curtius rearrangement describing thermal decomposition of acyl azides to isocyanates, which are then intercepted by water, amines, or alcohols to furnish amines, ureas, and carbamates. The page also compares routes from acid chlorides, mixed anhydrides, and carboxylic acids using azide-transfer reagents such as DPPA or trimethylsilyl azide.
notes: Key concepts: acyl azide formation, thermal loss of N2, isocyanate intermediates, amine/urea/carbamate product formation.

entities.notes: Named acyl azide to isocyanate rearrangement family overview.
Key reactive intermediate generated after nitrogen extrusion from the acyl azide.
Representative product class formed by trapping the isocyanate with an alcohol.

similar_existing.transformation_type: carboxylic acid derivative to isocyanate then amine / urea / carbamate
similar_existing.description_short: Thermal rearrangement of acyl azides to isocyanates, enabling conversion of carboxylic acids into amines, ureas, and carbamates.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acyl migration from phenolic esters or carbamates to aromatic carbon
similar_existing.description_short: Family of acyl-migration reactions that translocate acyl groups from oxygen to carbon on aromatic systems under ionic, radical, or anionic conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: allyl ether to allyl carbonyl or allyl phenol [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: allyl vinyl ether|allyl aryl ether|heat|Lewis acid variants
similar_existing.description_short: Thermal [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of allyl vinyl and allyl aryl ethers to gamma,delta-unsaturated carbonyl compounds or allyl phenols.

similar_existing.transformation_type: intramolecular aryl migration by nucleophilic aromatic substitution
similar_existing.description_short: Intramolecular rearrangement in which an anionic nucleophile attacks an activated aryl ring ipso-position to transfer the aryl group.

similar_existing.transformation_type: 4-carbonyl-substituted oxazole isomerization / heterocycle skeletal editing
similar_existing.description_short: Thermal rearrangement of 4-carbonyl-substituted oxazoles through dicarbonylnitrile ylide intermediates to isomeric oxazole products and related heterocycle-edited scaffolds.

title: Dakin Oxidation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Dakin oxidation describing conversion of ortho- or para-hydroxylated, alkoxylated, or amino-substituted aromatic aldehydes and ketones to substituted phenols using hydrogen peroxide or organic peracids under basic conditions through O-acylphenol intermediates.
notes: Key concepts: hydroperoxide addition, 1,2-aryl shift, O-acylphenol intermediates, substituted phenol formation from activated aromatic carbonyl compounds.

entities.notes: Named activated aryl carbonyl-to-phenol oxidation family overview.
Canonical rearrangement intermediate produced after hydroperoxide addition and 1,2-aryl shift.
Product class shown as the endpoint of the Dakin oxidation sequence.

similar_existing.transformation_type: activated aromatic aldehyde or ketone to substituted phenol
similar_existing.description_short: Oxidation/rearrangement of activated aromatic aldehydes or ketones to substituted phenols through peroxide addition and aryl migration.

similar_existing.transformation_type: oxidation of alcohols or oximes to carbonyl compounds with hypervalent iodine(V) reagent
similar_existing.description_short: Mild and chemoselective oxidation using Dess-Martin periodinane, a soluble hypervalent iodine(V) reagent derived from IBX, to convert alcohols and related substrates into carbonyl compounds.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd/Cu-catalyzed oxidation of alkenes to carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Palladium-catalyzed oxidation of alkenes, especially terminal alkenes, to carbonyl compounds including methyl ketones and related oxidative cyclization products.

similar_existing.transformation_type: buffered sodium chlorite oxidation of aldehydes to carboxylic acids
similar_existing.description_short: Reliable mild oxidation that converts aldehydes to carboxylic acids with sodium chlorite under buffered conditions while preserving many sensitive functional groups.

similar_existing.transformation_type: chromium(VI)-mediated oxidation of alcohols to carbonyl compounds or carboxylic acids
similar_existing.description_short: Chromium-based oxidation platform encompassing Jones reagent and related Cr(VI) reagents for the conversion of alcohols and related substrates into carbonyl compounds.

title: Dakin-West Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Dakin-West reaction describing conversion of alpha-amino acids into alpha-acylamino ketones by treatment with acid anhydrides and base through mixed anhydride and oxazolone intermediates, followed by acyl transfer and decarboxylation.
notes: Key concepts: amino-acid N-acylation, mixed anhydride formation, oxazolone intermediates, alpha-acylamino ketone formation.

entities.notes: Named amino-acid to alpha-acylamino ketone family overview.
Key cyclic intermediate highlighted in the mechanism of the Dakin-West reaction.
Canonical product class shown in the overview scheme.

similar_existing.transformation_type: alpha-amino acid to alpha-acylamino ketone
similar_existing.description_short: Transformation of alpha-amino acids into alpha-acylamino ketones under anhydride/base conditions through mixed anhydride and oxazolone intermediates.

similar_existing.transformation_type: iodide-mediated rearrangement or ring expansion of activated aziridines
similar_existing.description_short: Aziridine rearrangement family that converts N-acylaziridines into oxazolines and related heterocycles under nucleophilic conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acid-catalyzed coupling of alkenes with aldehydes to form oxocarbenium-derived oxygen heterocycles and alcohols
similar_existing.description_short: Versatile acid-promoted alkene–aldehyde coupling that channels oxocarbenium chemistry into tetrahydropyrans, 1,3-dioxanes, allylic alcohols, and related oxygenated products.

similar_existing.transformation_type: halogen exchange in alkyl halides
similar_existing.description_short: Classical SN2 halogen-exchange reaction in which sodium iodide or related salts convert alkyl halides or sulfonates into the corresponding iodides or bromides.

similar_existing.transformation_type: conversion of nitro compounds or nitronates into carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Classic nitro-to-carbonyl transformation that converts nitronate derivatives into aldehydes or ketones under acidic or related conditions.

title: Danheiser Benzannulation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Danheiser benzannulation describing conversion of cyclobutenones or alpha-diazoketones and activated heterosubstituted alkynes into highly substituted aromatic rings through vinylketene generation, cycloaddition, electrocyclic ring opening, and dienylketene rearrangement.
notes: Key concepts: cyclobutenone annulation, vinylketene intermediates, [2+2] cycloaddition/electrocyclic cascade, highly substituted aromatic ring synthesis.

entities.notes: Named cyclobutenone/diazoketone annulation family overview.
Key annulation intermediate generated from cyclobutenones or unsaturated alpha-diazoketones.
Canonical product class shown in the overview diagram.

similar_existing.description_short: Annulation method that converts cyclobutenone- or diazoketone-derived vinylketenes and activated alkynes into densely substituted aromatic rings.

similar_existing.description_short: TiCl4-promoted [3+2] annulation of trimethylsilylallenes with activated alkenes to furnish highly substituted cyclopentenes.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Michael addition followed by intramolecular aldol condensation to cyclohexenones

similar_existing.transformation_type: benzannulation of Fischer chromium carbene complexes with alkynes to hydroquinone / phenol derivatives
similar_existing.description_short: Chromium-carbene annulation with alkynes that forms aromatic rings, often hydroquinone derivatives, through a Dötz benzannulation sequence involving CO extrusion.

similar_existing.transformation_type: ortho formylation of phenols and heteroarenes via dichlorocarbene chemistry
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational dichlorocarbene-based formylation method for phenols and related heteroarenes that usually delivers ortho-aldehydes but can also produce abnormal rearrangement products.

title: Danheiser Cyclopentene Annulation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Danheiser cyclopentene annulation describing TiCl4-promoted [3+2] annulation of trimethylsilylallenes with electron-deficient alkenes (allenophiles) to form highly substituted cyclopentenes through allylic cation capture and 1,2-silyl shift pathways.
notes: Key concepts: TiCl4 activation of enones, trimethylsilylallene addition, allylic carbocation intermediates, 1,2-silyl shift, highly substituted cyclopentene formation.

entities.notes: Named annulation family overview.
Key cationic intermediate invoked after TiCl4 activation and allene addition.
Canonical product class shown in the overview scheme.

similar_existing.description_short: Annulation method that converts cyclobutenone- or diazoketone-derived vinylketenes and activated alkynes into densely substituted aromatic rings.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Michael addition followed by intramolecular aldol condensation to cyclohexenones
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational annulation strategy that builds cyclohexenones by combining Michael addition and intramolecular aldol chemistry.

title: Danishefsky's Diene Cycloaddition – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for Danishefsky's diene cycloaddition describing the electron-rich 1-methoxy-3-trimethylsilyloxy-1,3-butadiene as a versatile heterodiene in Lewis-acid-mediated or thermal [4+2] cycloadditions, often proceeding through Mukaiyama-aldol or Diels–Alder manifolds to oxygenated cyclohexenones and dihydropyrones.
notes: Key concepts: Danishefsky diene, hetero Diels–Alder cycloaddition, Mukaiyama aldol pathway, Lewis-acid activation, oxygenated six-membered rings.

entities.notes: Named heterodiene cycloaddition family overview.
Stepwise Lewis-acid-assisted pathway discussed alongside the concerted Diels–Alder manifold.
Characteristic oxygenated heterocycle formed from aldehyde partners.

similar_existing.description_short: Cycloaddition chemistry of Danishefsky's diene that rapidly builds oxygenated or heteroatom-containing six-membered rings from carbonyl, quinone, or imine partners.

similar_existing.description_short: Pericyclic or stepwise hetero-[4+2] annulation used to assemble densely functionalized oxygen- and nitrogen-heterocycles.

title: Davis' Oxaziridine Oxidations – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for Davis' oxaziridine oxidations describing N-sulfonyloxaziridines as neutral oxygen-transfer reagents for sulfide and selenide oxidation, amine N-oxidation, and especially alpha-hydroxylation of enolates to acyloins via SN2-type oxygen transfer.
notes: Key concepts: oxaziridine oxygen transfer, Davis reagent family, enolate alpha-hydroxylation, sulfide oxidation, non-chelated transition-state control.

entities.notes: Named oxaziridine oxygen-transfer family overview.
Three-membered N-O heterocycle oxygen-transfer reagent family highlighted on the page.

similar_existing.description_short: Mild and chemoselective oxidation using Dess-Martin periodinane, a soluble hypervalent iodine(V) reagent derived from IBX, to convert alcohols and related substrates into carbonyl compounds.

similar_existing.description_short: Low-temperature oxidation of alcohols with DMSO activated by oxalyl chloride or related reagents to give aldehydes or ketones.

title: Dienone-Phenol Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the dienone-phenol rearrangement describing acid- or base-promoted migration of alkyl groups in cyclohexadienones to aromatic phenols, including ortho- and para-dienone cases and sequential 1,2-shift pathways that deliver highly substituted phenols.
notes: Key concepts: dienone rearomatization, alkyl migration, ortho versus para substitution patterns, 1,2-shift cascades, acid-catalyzed rearrangement of santonin-type frameworks.

entities.notes: Named rearomatizing alkyl-migration family overview.
Overall migration pattern often rationalized as sequential 1,2-shifts in cyclohexadienone systems.
Representative rearomatized product class from para-dienone rearrangement.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acid-, base-, or photochemically induced alkyl migration in cyclohexadienones to substituted phenols
similar_existing.description_short: Rearrangement of cyclohexadienones to substituted phenols driven by rearomatization, often proceeding through sequential 1,2-shifts and sensitive to substitution pattern and reaction conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: tandem Prins cyclization and pinacol rearrangement to build tetrahydrofuran frameworks
similar_existing.description_short: Powerful tandem cationic cascade that merges Prins cyclization with pinacol rearrangement to forge densely substituted tetrahydrofuran and annulated ring systems.

similar_existing.transformation_type: ring-opening / ring-closing heterocycle isomerization with heteroatom translocation
similar_existing.description_short: Ring-opening / ring-closing rearrangement of heterocycles that translocates heteroatoms and attached substituents to deliver isomeric aza-heterocycles or rearranged nucleosides.

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-induced rearrangement of O-acyl ketoximes to alpha-amino ketones
similar_existing.description_short: Method for transforming oximes into alpha-amino ketones via O-acyl activation and alkoxide-promoted rearrangement.

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement of tertiary amine N-oxides to hydroxylamines or ring-expanded heterocycles
similar_existing.description_short: Rearrangement manifold of tertiary amine N-oxides that furnishes hydroxylamines or ring-expanded azaheterocycles via [1,2]- or [2,3]-shifts.

title: Dimroth Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Dimroth rearrangement describing ring-opening / ring-closing isomerization of heterocycles in which endocyclic or exocyclic heteroatoms and attached substituents are translocated, including Type I fused-ring and Type II exo/endocyclic heterocycle variants.
notes: Key concepts: ANRORC-type ring opening and reclosure, heteroatom translocation, nucleophile/base/heat promotion, and rearrangement control by ring size and substituent electronics.

entities.notes: Named heterocycle isomerization family overview.
Addition of nucleophile, ring opening, and ring closure sequence highlighted on the overview page.
Representative rearranged heteroaromatic product from the canonical Dimroth example.

similar_existing.transformation_type: ring-opening / ring-closing heterocycle isomerization with heteroatom translocation
similar_existing.description_short: Ring-opening / ring-closing rearrangement of heterocycles that translocates heteroatoms and attached substituents to deliver isomeric aza-heterocycles or rearranged nucleosides.

similar_existing.transformation_type: 4-carbonyl-substituted oxazole isomerization / heterocycle skeletal editing
similar_existing.description_short: Thermal rearrangement of 4-carbonyl-substituted oxazoles through dicarbonylnitrile ylide intermediates to isomeric oxazole products and related heterocycle-edited scaffolds.

similar_existing.transformation_type: anionic silyl migration / annulation
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: alkoxide or organolithium generation, acyl silanes, THF
similar_existing.description_short: Anionic migration of silyl groups from carbon to oxygen that enables rearrangement and annulation logic.

similar_existing.transformation_type: 1,5-diene [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement / ring expansion of strained dienes
similar_existing.description_short: Thermal reversible [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of 1,5-dienes that reorganizes carbon skeletons, including medium-ring-forming strained-substrate variants.

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: p-TsOH | Cc1ccc(S(=O)(=O)O)cc1; toluene | Cc1ccccc1; reflux | dry pyridine | c1ccncc1; AcOH | CC(=O)O; 24h, r.t.
similar_existing.description_short: Amadori Reaction

title: Doering-Laflamme Allene Synthesis – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Doering-Laflamme allene synthesis describing two-step one-carbon homologation of alkenes through dihalocarbene addition to form geminal dihalocyclopropanes followed by metal-mediated rearrangement to acyclic or cyclic allenes.
notes: Key concepts: in situ dihalocarbene generation from haloform, cyclopropanation of alkenes, metal–halogen exchange, and cyclopropylidene-to-allene rearrangement.

entities.notes: Named allene-synthesis family overview.
Key cyclopropane intermediate generated by dihalocarbene addition.
Canonical cyclic allene product class shown in the overview scheme.

similar_existing.transformation_type: one-carbon homologation of alkenes to allenes via dihalocyclopropane intermediates
similar_existing.description_short: Classical allene synthesis in which dihalocarbene addition to an alkene gives a geminal dihalocyclopropane that is rearranged under metal-mediated conditions to an allene.

similar_existing.transformation_type: aldehyde one-carbon homologation to terminal alkyne
similar_existing.description_short: One-carbon homologation of aldehydes to terminal alkynes through dibromoolefin intermediates and organolithium-mediated elimination.

similar_existing.transformation_type: enamine-mediated alpha-functionalization of carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Use of enamines as enolate equivalents for alpha-alkylation, acylation, Michael addition, and related carbonyl functionalization.

similar_existing.transformation_type: controlled acyl substitution to ketones or aldehydes
similar_existing.description_short: Preparation and use of N-methoxy-N-methylamides to convert carboxylic acid derivatives into ketones or aldehydes with single-addition control.

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-promoted cyclization of N-acyl-o-toluidines or related anilides to indoles
similar_existing.description_short: Classical and modified intramolecular anilide cyclization methods for building 2-substituted and 2,3-disubstituted indoles.

title: Dötz Benzannulation Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Dötz benzannulation reaction describing formal [3+2+1] annulation of Fischer-type chromium carbene complexes with alkynes to furnish hydroquinone or phenol derivatives after carbon monoxide loss and benzannulation.
notes: Key concepts: chromium Fischer carbene preparation, alkyne insertion, carbon monoxide extrusion, regiochemical control of benzannulation, and oxidative unveiling of hydroquinone products.

entities.notes: Named chromium-carbene benzannulation family overview.
Chromium carbene intermediate central to the Dötz benzannulation.
Canonical aromatic product class produced after benzannulation.

similar_existing.transformation_type: benzannulation of Fischer chromium carbene complexes with alkynes to hydroquinone / phenol derivatives
similar_existing.description_short: Chromium-carbene annulation with alkynes that forms aromatic rings, often hydroquinone derivatives, through a Dötz benzannulation sequence involving CO extrusion.

similar_existing.transformation_type: tertiary amine N-oxide syn elimination to alkene and hydroxylamine
similar_existing.description_short: Thermal syn elimination of tertiary amine N-oxides to alkenes and dialkylhydroxylamines through a cyclic transition state.

similar_existing.transformation_type: cyclobutenone or diazoketone annulation with activated alkyne to substituted arene
similar_existing.description_short: Annulation method that converts cyclobutenone- or diazoketone-derived vinylketenes and activated alkynes into densely substituted aromatic rings.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acid-catalyzed coumarin synthesis
similar_existing.description_short: Acid-catalyzed condensation of phenols with β-keto esters or related partners to furnish coumarins.

similar_existing.transformation_type: hydroboration / hydroboration-oxidation
similar_existing.description_short: Regio- and stereoselective addition of borane to alkenes or alkynes to form versatile organoboranes.

title: Enders SAMP/RAMP Hydrazone Alkylation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Enders SAMP/RAMP hydrazone alkylation describing asymmetric alpha-alkylation of ketones or aldehydes through chiral SAMP/RAMP hydrazone auxiliaries, azaenolate formation, and stereoselective electrophile addition.
notes: Key concepts: hydrazone formation from carbonyl compounds, LDA-mediated deprotonation, chiral auxiliary-controlled alkylation, and regeneration / diversification of the alkylated carbonyl products.

entities.notes: Named chiral hydrazone alkylation family overview.
Auxiliary-bearing hydrazone intermediate used for diastereoselective alpha-functionalization.
Reactive lithiated intermediate that governs stereoselective alkylation.

similar_existing.transformation_type: auxiliary-controlled asymmetric alpha-alkylation of carbonyl compounds via chiral hydrazones
similar_existing.description_short: Asymmetric alpha-functionalization strategy that uses chiral SAMP or RAMP hydrazones to direct diastereoselective alkylation of aldehydes and ketones.

similar_existing.transformation_type: ene reaction / allylic transposition addition
similar_existing.description_short: The Alder ene reaction is a pericyclic addition of an allylic alkene to an enophile with concomitant allylic hydrogen transfer and double-bond migration.

similar_existing.transformation_type: diastereoselective alpha-alkylation of pseudoephedrine-derived amide enolates
similar_existing.description_short: Chiral auxiliary-based alkylation method that enables highly selective construction of alpha-substituted carboxylic acid derivatives.
